title: 4 Installing a Soft Token Authentication App and Registering a Soft Token
body_text:
4	Installing a Soft Token Authentication App and Registering a Soft Token
4.1	Installing a Soft Token Authentication App
Users assigned a soft token must install an RFC 6238 security standard compliant authenticator app on the their mobile device. Central 1 recommends Google Authenticator; however, several other authenticator apps are available.
Download authenticator apps from the Google Play Store (for Android devices) or the Apple Store (for iPhones).
4.2	Registering a Soft Token
Users assigned a soft token must register it within 14 calendar days of the assignment date. See the User Management Part for procedures on assigning a soft token. Users are prompted to register their 2-step security token the first time they access certain applications (e.g., User Management), or functionality within an application (e.g., approving outgoing wires in the Wires application) thatâs protected by 2-step security.
When prompted to register a soft token:
Select
Enable
on the Register 2-Step Security page. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Register a 2-Step Security Soft Token
Confirm that a compliant authenticator app is installed on your mobile device (see Section 4.1, Installing a Soft Token Authentication App for more information), then select
Next
on the Enable 2-Step Security page. See Figure 2.
The following instructions apply to Google Authenticator. If youâre using a different authenticator app, the process to add a token may differ slightly.
Figure 2: Select Next on the Enable 2-Step Security Page
Add a soft token to Google Authenticator as follows:
Open the authenticator app on your mobile device. Ensure that itâs allowed to access your camera.
Select the plus icon (+), then select
Scan QR Code
. See Figure 3. The camera on your mobile device is activated.
Figure 3: Add QR Code
Position your mobile deviceâs camera over the QR code displayed on the Enable 2-Step Security page on your computer screen. Align the QR code within the green box on your phone. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Scan QR Code
Enter a 6-digit code generated in the authenticator app in the âEnter Codeâ field on the Enable 2-Step Security page, then select
Next
. See Figure 5 and Figure 6.
Figure 5: 6-digit 2-Step Security Code
Figure 6: Enter the 6-digit 2-Step Security Code
Select
Done
to complete the soft token registration. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Select Done
4.3	Replacing a Soft Token
If a user requires a replacement soft token (for example, the user has a new mobile device), a User Management Security Officer must remove the old soft token from the userâs profile in the User Management application, then assign the user a new one. See the User Management Part for procedures. Have the user delete the old token from their authenticator app.
Note:	Users assigned a replacement soft token must register it within 14 business days of the assignment date. See Section 4.2, Registering a Soft Token for more information.
END
